results.possibleIdentity: Robert Friedman was an engineer and technical executive active in the late 1960s and early 1970s, specializing in large-screen and closed-circuit television (CCTV) technologies. He joined TNT Communications, Inc. (also known as Theatre Network Television) in 1968 as the Director of Marketing and was subsequently appointed Vice-President and General Manager of its Electronics Division. His primary technical focus was the Eidophor, a control-layer television projector used for high-resolution, high-brightness large-screen displays in fields such as visual simulation and public events. He authored the technical paper "The Eidophor - A Control Layer Television Projector," presented at the 1969 SPIE seminar on Photo-Optical Techniques in Simulators. Prior to his tenure at TNT, Friedman held significant engineering and management roles at Dalto Electronics Corp. (serving as VP and General Manager), the Link Group of General Precision Inc., and the Kollsman Instrument Corp. He is associated with Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that fostered collaborations between artists and engineers, and was based out of Spring Valley, New York, while TNT Communications maintained offices in Woodside, New York.
